mnemonics: Try-Catch-Finally: Try to Catch errors, Finally clean up!
keyPoints: Exceptions handle runtime errors, separating error-handling code from regular code.
Checked exceptions must be handled or declared; unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException and its subclasses) do not.
Use try-with-resources for AutoCloseable objects to ensure they are closed automatically.
commonMistakes: Catching Exception instead of more specific exception types, which can mask bugs.
Using exceptions for normal control flow, which is inefficient and makes code harder to read.
Swallowing exceptions (empty catch block) without logging or handling them, making debugging impossible.
